key_departures.impact: Franchise icon and all-time Bucs leader in receiving yards and TDs departs after 12 seasons. Was limited to 8 games in 2025 (broken clavicle), recording 30 catches/368 yards. Decision was not about money — Bucs made aggressive offer but Evans wanted a new challenge. Leaves a significant void as WR1, though Emeka Egbuka (2025 2nd-round pick), Chris Godwin, Jalen McMillan, and Tez Johnson remain.

contract_signed_elsewhere.guaranteed_note: Guarantees not yet fully reported; contract announced by Tom Pelissero/Ian Rapoport

key_departures.impact: Starting outside CB departs after 7 seasons. Had a career-best 2025 (3 INTs, 9 PDs, sub-50% completion rate allowed, 86.9 PFF coverage grade). Bucs let him out of his contract early — now it backfires. Remaining CBs are Benjamin Morrison (rookie), Zyon McCollum, and Jacob Parrish. No proven veteran CB1 remains on the roster.

Starting RB alongside Bucky Irving departs. White was RB1 in 2023-24 before Irving took over. Was a versatile 3-down back. Departure expected; Buccaneers immediately replaced him with Kenneth Gainwell signing.

One-year prove-it signing ($14M/1yr in 2025) failed to deliver — 2.5 sacks in 13 games, missed time with ankle/knee injuries. Was never truly the edge rusher they needed. Departure leaves the EDGE room with Yaya Diaby as the only reliable pass rusher; Chris Braswell has not developed, David Walker coming off torn ACL.

2022 first-round pick just completed his rookie deal. Started 16 games in 2025, career-high 39 tackles. Potential free agency departure would create a hole at IDL alongside Vita Vea. Status as of March 10 unresolved — remains unsigned.

Franchise legend has not yet decided on retirement or return. Was still a starter in 2025 (114 tackles, 3.5 sacks, 97.99% snap share) but PFF ranked him 66th/88 qualifying LBs. Has said it's Bucs or nowhere — decision remains pending as of March 10. Anzalone signing partially hedges against his departure.

signings.analysis: 3yr/$30M with $20M guaranteed is a real investment — this is their TE for the foreseeable future. Otton had back-to-back 59-catch seasons (572 yards in 2025, 600 yards in 2024) and is the most durable TE in the NFL over the past 4 years. $10M AAV sits in the starter-tier range for the position. The Bucs prioritized this signing on Day 1 of legal tampering, suggesting confidence in Otton as their anchor on offense with Evans departing. TE is fully addressed — should not be a draft priority.

2yr/$14M ($10M guaranteed) is a legitimate investment at RB. Gainwell had a breakout 2025 with the Steelers: 1,023 scrimmage yards, 8 TDs, 73 catches on 85 targets, earning team MVP honors. At 26, he's ascending. Will pair with Bucky Irving (the clear RB1) to form a versatile two-headed committee. $7M AAV sits squarely in starter territory for an every-down back. The Gainwell signing directly replaces Rachaad White and gives Baker Mayfield a dangerous receiving option out of the backfield. RB need is addressed — depth will be handled via Tucker/Josh Williams.

contract.guaranteed_note: Guarantees not yet confirmed; reported by Spotrac as 2yr/$17M AAV $8.5M

signings.analysis: 2yr/$17M at $8.5M AAV is mid-tier starter money for a LB — reasonable investment for a proven, durable starter. Anzalone had 95 tackles, 2.5 sacks, 9 PDs, and 1 INT in 2025 for Detroit, starting all 16 games and serving as a 5-time team captain. He reportedly turned down more money to come to Tampa (Florida tax benefit + competitive team). At 31, this is a bridge signing — 2 years of effective guarantees on a 2-year deal. Anzalone fills the void if Lavonte David retires, but LB remains a need for the future (SirVocea Dennis has not proven himself as an every-down LB, and the Bucs still need a long-term solution). Need drops from critical to moderate — still a strong draft target in Rounds 2-4.
Veteran minimum QB signing — pure depth/camp competition behind Baker Mayfield. No need impact whatsoever.

contract.guaranteed_note: Terms undisclosed; standard long snapper deal

signings.analysis: Roster maintenance re-signing of their long snapper. No impact on offensive or defensive needs.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Mike Evans signed with the 49ers (3yr/$42.4M, $16.3M guaranteed). The Bucs' WR room is now Godwin (injury history, 31 years old in 2026), Emeka Egbuka (promising 2025 rookie cut short by injury), Jalen McMillan, and Tez Johnson. No proven WR1 remains on the roster. Godwin is a WR1-caliber talent but cannot be relied upon for a full 17-game season. This is now Tampa's most urgent offensive need alongside EDGE. Should target WR in Rounds 1-2 of the draft.

Jamel Dean signed with the Steelers (3yr/$36.75M) on Day 1 of free agency, creating a new hole. The Bucs now enter 2026 with Benjamin Morrison (2025 2nd-round pick, unproven), Zyon McCollum, and Jacob Parrish (2025 Day 3 pick) as their CBs. No veteran starting-caliber CB remains on the roster. This need was created entirely by free agency. CB must be addressed in the draft (Rounds 1-3) or via another free agent signing in the coming weeks.

Haason Reddick (2.5 sacks in 13 games) departs, having failed to address the need he was brought in for. Yaya Diaby is a solid EDGE2 at best ($20-22M market value), not a true alpha. Chris Braswell has not developed. David Walker is recovering from a torn ACL. Trey Hendrickson interest was reported but his price ($25M+/yr) is likely too high for Tampa's cap situation. EDGE remains the defining defensive need heading into the draft — highest-priority defensive position. Should target EDGE in Round 1 or early Round 2.

Logan Hall (2022 1st-round pick, 25 years old) remains unsigned and in free agency as of March 10. If Hall departs, IDL escalates to tier 1 alongside EDGE. Vita Vea (30) is under contract but aging and expensive. Elijah Roberts is developing but unproven as a starter. Recommend monitoring Hall's situation closely — his potential departure is the x-factor for this position group.

Alex Anzalone signing (2yr/$17M) partially addresses the LB need. He is a proven starter who brings leadership and durability from 5 years in Detroit. However, Lavonte David's retirement/status is unresolved, SirVocea Dennis has not proven himself, and Anzalone at 31 is not a long-term solution. LB remains a Rounds 2-4 draft priority — team needs a future every-down linebacker to develop.

Cade Otton re-signed on a 3yr/$30M deal with $20M guaranteed. He is their clear starter and the contract structure signals a 3-year commitment. He had back-to-back 59-catch seasons and is the anchor of Zac Robinson's offense. TE is no longer a need. At most, a late-round developmental pick could be considered, but it should not consume draft capital.

Gainwell signing (2yr/$14M, $10M gtd) plus Bucky Irving (their clear RB1) gives the Bucs one of the better backfield committees in the NFC. Gainwell's pass-catching ability (73 catches in 2025) directly replaces White's role. RB should not be a priority in this draft. Only late-round depth worth considering.

key_additions.headline: Steelers' 2025 Team MVP (1,023 scrimmage yards, 73 catches, 8 TDs) joins Bucky Irving to form one of the NFL's most versatile backfield combos
5-time team captain and proven starter from Detroit's defense takes over middle linebacker duties, addressing Tampa's biggest defensive need
Re-signed tight end on 3-year deal provides offensive continuity as the Bucs' primary target under new OC Zac Robinson after back-to-back 59-catch seasons

key_losses.headline: Franchise legend and all-time Bucs receiving leader departs after 12 seasons, creating an immediate WR1 void that must be addressed in the 2026 NFL Draft
Starting outside CB leaves after a career-best 2025 season, creating a new hole in a secondary now relying heavily on unproven young players
Versatile 3-down back departs after 4 seasons; immediately replaced by Kenneth Gainwell signing
One-year prove-it deal (2.5 sacks in 13 games) fails to deliver; EDGE remains the Bucs' most critical defensive need entering the 2026 draft

notes: All signings confirmed via ESPN (Schefter/Fowler), NFL Network (Pelissero/Rapoport), and Spotrac as of March 10, 2026. Contracts can be officially signed Wednesday March 11 at 4pm ET.
Logan Hall UFA status unresolved as of March 10 — his departure would escalate IDL from tier 2 to tier 1.
Lavonte David retirement/return decision still pending as of March 10 — if he retires, LB should escalate back to tier 1.
Alex Anzalone guarantee structure not yet fully confirmed; 2yr/$17M AAV from Spotrac is verified, guarantees TBD.
Jamel Dean guarantee structure not yet confirmed; total value of $36.75M over 3 years confirmed by Pelissero/Rapoport.

Pre-FA cap space reflected as OTC figure ($19.96M); Spotrac had a lower figure ($11.3M) — likely reflects different restructure assumptions. Post-FA estimate is approximate based on reported deal totals.
